claim: Free-roaming dogs exhibit a crepuscular activity pattern with daily peaks in roaming distance in the early morning and late afternoon/evening, and reduced movement during midday heat.
stance_target: heat and temperature effects on activity cycle — dogs avoid midday heat
scope_conditions: 132 GPS-tracked free-roaming dogs in Torres Strait and Northern Peninsula Area communities, northern Australia
quoted_support: Daily peaks in mean roaming distance were observed twice per day — early morning and late afternoon/evening — consistent with crepuscular activity pattern.
region: Torres Strait and Northern Peninsula Area, Australia (tropical)

claim: Free-roaming dogs roam significantly farther during the dry season than the wet season, with season being a statistically significant risk factor for distance from residence.
stance_target: Algarve hot dry summer produces expanded roaming range for lost dogs
scope_conditions: 132 GPS-tracked dogs, Torres Strait and Northern Peninsula Area, northern Australia
quoted_support: Dogs roamed furthest in the NPA and during the dry season.
region: Torres Strait, Northern Peninsula Area, Australia

claim: Sex, age, season, and hour of day are all statistically significant predictors of roaming distance in free-roaming dogs.
stance_target: multi-factor model for lost dog movement prediction
scope_conditions: 132 dogs, GPS regression analysis, northern Australia
quoted_support: The influence of risk factors (including age, sex, location, season and hour of day) on dogs' distance from their residences were assessed.
